Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(1998) (Gorillaz is formed by Damon Albarn & Jamie Hewlett)
(December 12, 2001) (Gorillaz & G-Sides is officially released in the UK
Type: The first album by Gorillaz following the Tomorrow Comes Today EP. The album was made in collaboration with Deltron 3030 members Dan the Automator (also of Handsome Boy Modelling School, Lovage, etc.) and Kid Koala, with Del the Funky Homosapien featuring on "Clint Eastwood" and "Rock the House".

Name:
Demon Days
(prod. Gorillaz & Danger Mouse)
(Celebrity Harvest, We Are Happy Landfill)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(March 26, 2001) & (December 12, 2001) (Gorillaz & G-Sides are officially released)
(May 11, 2005) & (November 19, 2007) (Demon Days & D-Sides are officially released)
Type: With the goal of proving Gorillaz wasn’t a gimmick, Demon Days featured a noticeable shift in sound from the dub-influenced underground hip-hop of Gorillaz to a more political rock, pop, choral and hip-hop route brought on by string arrangements, drum-sequencing, and lyrics crticizing the Afghanistan War and life post-911. Co-produced by then-newcomer Danger Mouse, Demon Days is also the first Gorillaz album to heavily feature other artists and rely on collaboration, a future mainstay for all Gorillaz projects.

(May 11, 2005) & (November 19, 2007) (Demon Days & D-Sides officially releases)
(August 18, 2008) (Monkey - Journey to the West officially releases)
Type: "Monkey - Journey To The West" is an opera that is a stage adaptation of the Chinese novel "Journey To The West," made in collaboration with Chinese actor and director Chen Shi-Zheng in 2005. Damon and Hewlett have both referred to Monkey: Journey To The West as a Gorillaz project. Originally, they intended for the soundtrack to be released as a Gorillaz project, under the Gorillaz name, but were unable to do so for legal reasons.

Name: Carousel
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(November 2007) (Work on Carousel begins)
(June 2008) (Recording for Plastic Beach begins)
(November 2009) (Carousel is scrapped)
Type: Announced in 2008 as the third Gorillaz album, Carousel was to be a multi-media orchestral art-rock project contained in 15 stories. Thematically, Carousel would revolve around the mystical aspects of Britain and the progression of life all taking place on a 100-mile pier. Carousel was scrapped by EMI who refused to fund the project while Damon and Jamie struggled to structure the project. Damon and Jamie would then carry over elements from Carousel to another Gorillaz project, Plastic Beach.

Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(January 14, 2009) (Damon plays three demos on BBC Radio 1)
(March 3, 2010) (Plastic Beach officially releases)
Type: Conceived initially as three separate projects: Carousel, Plastic Beach and Dee, The Opera. The third Gorillaz album took on a more environmental focus from Damon observing the amounts of plastic on the sands of a beach. Plastic Beach is infamous for going over the band's budget, and its scrapped content, leading to the 2018 #FreeTheSea campaign calling for the release of the 200+ unreleased songs recorded during the making of Phase 3.

Name: Meanwhile
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord:
(October 23, 2020) (Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez officially releases)
(August 26, 2021) (Meanwhile officially releases)
Type: The existence of new Gorillaz material after Song Machine, Season One: Strange Timez would be revealed by Damon in an interview with NME describing it as "carnival-themed". Damon would later reveal in an interview with Mark Ronson that a new Gorillaz EP would be released to compliment that year's Notting Hill Carnival. Meanwhile was also released in celebration as a part of the 20th anniversary of Gorillaz, showcasing a modern take on the sounds explored 20 years ago on Gorillaz.

Not just an album, but bigger." Cracker Island would topically revolve around cults and the Los Angeles lifestyle, where the album was recorded in 9-11 days with album collaborator Greg Kurstin.

Type: Vaguely hinted at in their 2023 Coachella interview, the ninth Gorillaz album will be recorded somewhere they'd "never been before", likely Mumbai as teased in recent social media posts. Several tracks from the album were played at a mystery show on September 3rd, 2025. It is very likely this new album is in some way a spiritual successor to Plastic Beach, having several unused tracks from the era repurposed.

Name:
The Mountain ⛰️
(feat. Dennis Hopper, Ajay Prasanna, Anousha Shankar, Amaan Ali Bangash & Ayaan Ali Bangash)
Notes (Join the Gorillaz Tracker Discord: Track 1 from the Mountain is the opening instrumental sequence of this album, inspiration came from Damon Albarn recording a Rajasthan folk tune played on the outside of the Amber Fort. After recording, Damon went back to his hotel and messed around with it to get the first demo which he would send to Jamie Hewlett. Later they would get artists Anousha Shankar, Ajay Prasanna, Amaan & Ali Bangash to contribute to the track and then would reuse outtakes from "Fire Coming Out of a Monkey's Head" to get Dennis Hopper's voice lines. This song would later be used in the Gorillaz short film as a medley with The Moon Cave and the Sad God.
